turns.user_message: I walked into the grocery store yesterday and just stood frozen in the doorway for a solid minute. It used to be completely fine, but suddenly the fluorescent lights felt way too bright and the background chatter was overwhelming. My chest got tight and I had to grip the cart handle just to steady myself. I ended up grabbing a few things and rushing back out before I could even finish my list. It really caught me off guard how quickly my whole body reacted to something so ordinary.

Since that trip, I keep finding myself hesitating right outside the automatic doors every single time I go out. What used to be a quick errand now feels like I am walking into a storm. I spend so much energy just trying to pace my breathing before I even step over the threshold. My heart starts pounding the second I hear the hum of the freezers, and I catch myself scanning for the nearest exit. It is exhausting to feel like my own nervous system is constantly on high alert over something so routine.

Now I avoid going anywhere with too many people because the panic just hits me out of nowhere. I stood near the entrance again last night and my vision actually started to blur while my hands went completely numb. I had to lean against the wall and count backwards just to keep from bolting right out the door. It feels like my brain is stuck in permanent overdrive, turning every crowded space into a minefield I cannot navigate. I am so tired of feeling trapped in my own head whenever I try to do normal things.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by referencing a specific recent moment. This used to not bother the speaker.
